title: Algorithm for Gestational Age Assessment at Birth
author: Zilma Reis
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bawbifan

steps.title: The last menstrual period reference The...
steps.instruction: The last menstrual period reference The interview aims to qualify the information about the last menstrual period, in terms of its reliability.

steps.title: When did your last menstrual period occur
steps.instruction: When did your last menstrual period occur? Please, confirm if it was the first day of menstruation. Question

steps.title: Are you sure about this date
steps.instruction: Are you sure about this date? Question

steps.title: Are your menstrual cycles regulars
steps.instruction: Are your menstrual cycles regulars? Clarify her that it was when the duration of the cycles did not change for more than one week. Question

steps.title: Two months before your last menstrual...
steps.instruction: Two months before your last menstrual period, did you use any contraceptive method, like pills, injections, Mirena IUD, skin implant, vaginal ring? Question

steps.title: Two months before your last menstrual...
steps.instruction: Two months before your last menstrual period, had you an abortion or delivered a child?

steps.title: For further double-check, take a picture...
steps.instruction: For further double-check, take a picture of the personal prenatal care book where the last menstrual period is reported. Make sure she permitted you for this step. Make sure the informed date is the same reported in the personal prenatal care book, to avoid memory recall bias.

steps.title: The first obstetric ultrasound reference This...
steps.instruction: The first obstetric ultrasound reference This step aims to assess the best antenatal ultrasound record to assist the gestational age calculation at birth. When the assessment occurred between 7w+0d and 13w+6d of pregnancy, the crow-rump-length (CRL) measurement of the embryo is the primary reference. The research should certify the source of information, preferably the value of CRL.

steps.title: Chronologically organize the antenatal obstetric ultrasound reports
steps.instruction: Chronologically organize the antenatal obstetric ultrasound reports. Select the first gestational assessment with an available CRL measurement, among 7w+0d and 13w+6d of pregnancy. Take note of the assessment date and the reported gestational age at this time. When gestational age is incomplete as 7/8 weeks, consider 3 days: 7w+3d.Take note of the CRL measurement, when available even in the embryo image. Crown-rump-length (CRL) measurementGestational age will be adjusted according to Intergrowth’s 21st standard curve for ultrasound CRL measurements using the International Fetal Size in Early Pregnancy from 7 weeks and 3 days up to 13 weeks and 6 days (Papageorghiou AT et al., 2014).

steps.title: Take a picture of the reference...
steps.instruction: Take a picture of the reference ultrasound report if available or the personal prenatal care book where the ultrasound result is reported. Make sure she permitted you for this step.

steps.title: DOI :10

steps.title: Methods for estimating the due date
steps.instruction: Methods for estimating the due date. Obstet Gynecol. 2017;129(5):e150-

steps.title: DetailsInsert data in the information system entries
steps.instruction: DetailsInsert data in the information system entries: %s %s

steps.title: A team of researchers-experts has a...
steps.instruction: A team of researchers-experts has a role in rechecking the data, according to sources as photos of ultrasound reports, the digital image of the CRL, the digital copies of the personal prenatal care book. This procedure demands access to the database simultaneously with the digital images. After one-by-one of the newborns' data double-checking, gestational age obtained at birth should be adjusted to mirror the registered information when the original data was recollected. Once more, the algorithm will process the optimal dating gestational age at birth.

steps.title: We expect the best accuracy for...
steps.instruction: We expect the best accuracy for the gestational age assessment at birth.
